title: camphusia glabra
intro: Camphusia glabra, also known as the West Indian gourd or calabash, is a type of vine plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help treat various health issues. The health benefits of Camphusia glabra include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and support immune system function. In herbal preparations, Camphusia glabra is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including wounds and skin conditions.
